NLP Survey 2023 — State of the Field: This survey reviews major advances in natural language processing from 2020–2023, with particular focus on large language models (LLMs) and their emergent capabilities. GPT-4, PaLM-2, Claude, and LLaMA-2 represent the frontier of general-purpose language models, demonstrating strong performance across reasoning, coding, and creative tasks. Instruction tuning and reinforcement learning from human feedback (RLHF) have proven critical for aligning LLM outputs with human intent and improving safety. Open challenges include reliable factuality (reducing hallucination), long-context coherence, mathematical reasoning, and reducing the computational cost of inference for real-time applications.
